definition:
κακία, -ας, ἡ
 (κακός), [in LXX chiefly for רָאָה ;] 
  1. badness in quality (opposite to ἀρετή, excellence). 
   2. wickedness, depravity, malignity: Act.8:22, Rom.1:29, 1Co.5:8 14:20, Eph.4:31, Col.3:8, Tit.3:3, Jas.1:21, 1Pe.2:1 2:16. 
   3. In late Gk., evil, trouble, affliction (Amo.3:6, 1Ki.6:9, Ecc.7:15, Sir.19:6, al.): Mat.6:34 (cf. MM, Exp., xv).†
 SYN.: (πονηρία) (AS)
